meaning: n.突然下降,猛扑vt攫取vi.飞扑,突然袭击
examples: The hawk poised in mid-air ready to swoop.
A swoop by customs officers netted a large quantity of drugs.
It seemed that the plane was going to swoop down and strafe the town, so we dived for cover.
Gymnasts swoop through the air on zip wires and are catapulted out of cannons.
Some small-market owner will cash out, and Jordan will swoop in as managing partner of an ownership group.
